memo: The draft decision to approve cardiac rehabilitation for 36 sessions is supported. File is complete with qualifying event documentation and physician referral. Member experienced a drug-eluting stent placement for NSTEMI on 2026-06-12, which is within the twelve-month qualifying window and constitutes a covered qualifying event under MCR-CR. Cardiologist prescription for supervised cardiac rehabilitation with risk-factor modification and outcomes assessment is present. The requested 36 sessions fall within the coverage limit of up to 36 supervised sessions per MCR-CR. No compliance issues identified. Clock is healthy (97 hours remaining). This decision may be finalized.
member_impact: Your request for cardiac rehabilitation has been approved. Your doctor prescribed 36 supervised sessions to help your heart recover after your stent placement. These sessions will include exercise, education about heart health, and checks on how you're doing. Medicare will cover this program.
recommended_actions: Finalize approval notice and route to member.
Confirm cardiac rehab provider is in-network; if not, apply network rules per plan documents.

citations.quote: Cardiac rehabilitation programs are covered for members who within the preceding twelve months have experienced a heart attack, coronary artery bypass surgery, stable angina, heart valve repair or replacement, coronary angioplasty or stenting, or a heart or heart-lung transplant. Coverage includes up to 36 supervised sessions over up to 36 weeks, with physician-prescribed exercise, risk-factor modification, and outcomes assessment.
A cardiac rehabilitation review requires documentation of the qualifying cardiac event within the preceding twelve months and the physician referral prescribing the program.

findings.detail: File is complete: all 2 required documents for cardiac_rehab are present.
Decision clock: 97 hours remain on the standard track (due 2026-07-14T10:00:00Z).
